description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the speckled, brownish stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a uniform dark coloration, suggesting a dense and heavy construction. The top surface appears rough and textured, likely to provide traction or prevent slipping. The block is thick and squat, with a low profile relative to its width and length. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a curb, step, or protective edge.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its rough, speckled top surface and solid dark sides. The grooved pattern provides a striking visual contrast and likely enhances traction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal grooves running across the top surface, with the solid dark face of the block visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's length emphasized and the dark side panel visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the parallel grooves and the solid base of the block.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the lengthwise grooves and the block's substantial thickness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-defined, with no signs of erosion or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous barrier or curb
